testEnvironment.testTool: Playwright Chromium with WebAuthn virtual authenticator

notes: VAL-CROSS-101: First-time registration lands in authenticated shell - verified via 3 Playwright tests
VAL-CROSS-102: Returning login lands in authenticated shell - verified via 2 Playwright tests
VAL-CROSS-103: Protected shell transport uses cookie-backed auth only - verified via 3 Playwright tests (no localStorage/sessionStorage tokens, no bearer headers, no direct service port calls)
VAL-CROSS-104: Expired access renews without new passkey ceremony - verified via 2 Playwright tests
VAL-CROSS-105: Reload/new-tab restart rehydrates from server-backed auth state - verified via 2 Playwright tests
VAL-CROSS-106: Logout revokes shell and protected live surfaces - verified via 4 Playwright tests (WebSocket teardown, protected route denial, no shell resurrection)
VAL-CROSS-107: User switch does not leak stale shell state - verified via 1 Playwright test
VAL-CROSS-108: Failed renewal falls back cleanly to guest state - verified via 3 Playwright tests
VAL-CROSS-118: Auth and proxy restarts recover safely - verified via code review and architecture (NixOS/systemd restart paths configured, services healthy after start)
All 61 total Playwright tests passed across 7 test files, providing comprehensive coverage of deploy-readiness browser contract
